overall_feedback: Round 3 audit after substantive new content (C4 discussion paragraph, C5 appendix subsection with new asymmetric-Dirichlet table). Integration is clean. Robustness data matches the CSV outputs to displayed precision. No regressions on math or numerical claims. One Oxford-UK spelling lapse ('realisations' in the new C4 paragraph) — the same class of regression as round 2's M2 in §3.4, two-line fix.

comments.title: Oxford-UK spelling regression in new C4 paragraph
comments.quote: Hedging-style behaviour aimed at balancing payoffs across realisations is structurally similar.
comments.explanation: Paper is standardised on Oxford UK: -ize and -our. 'realisations' is British -ise and conflicts with 'realizations'/'realized' used throughout 01-, 02-, 03-, and elsewhere in 07_.

comments.title: New C4 paragraph makes one mechanism claim slightly stronger than the underlying analysis
comments.quote: This moves the squared-distance identified set the most, because that set is the projection polytope of the reported count vector and shifts continuously with the reported coordinates; for frequency-guessing the report is a multinomial mode, so discrete jumps in the report can leave the identified set unchanged across a range of true beliefs.
comments.explanation: Phrase 'moves the squared-distance identified set the most' is a comparative claim across rules; the rest of the paragraph supports a directional/qualitative ordering, not a strict 'most'. A referee may read it as overclaim relative to the testable-predictions framing.
comments.fix: 07_discussion.tex:116 — soften 'moves the squared-distance identified set the most' to 'moves the squared-distance identified set more than the frequency-guessing identified set'.
